score_note: Accurately explains dominance and the cell/room/door cardinality refinements.
Mentions minimum, image, and cardinality bound, but incorrectly overstates the image as a bijection/cardinality equality.
Correctly distinguishes point insertion and index insertion, but does not explain incidence counting.
Correctly distinguishes colorful, nearly colorful, and typed missing-color versions and connects typing to counting.
Identifies room_of_colorful as a prerequisite for pick_colorful_point, but gives an imprecise cardinality/nonempty explanation.
Correctly explains typed door-room incidence and the odd/even partition supporting existence.
Correctly explains the singleton outside-door filter and odd cardinality.
Gets parity-to-existence, but misstates part of the parity equation and does not cleanly identify the outside/internal split.
Correctly states colorful Nonempty and explains Inhabited I/default as the selected missing type.
Correctly connects Fcolor, grid levels, Scarf, room_seq, and room_point_seq.
Recognizes finite-image subsequence extraction, but treats the stabilized object as a single color rather than the room color set C.
Explains outside-C coordinates tending to zero, but does not state how this combines with coordinate inequalities.
Mentions compactness and shrinking diameter, but misses the transfer-of-inequalities role.
Mentions limit, continuity, and coordinate inequality, but omits colored witnesses and overgeneralizes the inequality.
Output is truncated almost immediately and does not answer the proof-strategy question.
Explains deficit/tPush/pushTowardsZ broadly, but misses that the key purpose is positive blockSum for normalization and overstates exact block correction.
Explains projection-after-embedding as a retraction, but does not spell out the lifted map fixed-point conversion.
Starts a correct f_lifted/Brouwer/project-back summary, but truncates before the project_embed_id conclusion.
Explains finite mixed-game representation and expected payoff, but omits the unilateral-deviation Nash condition.
Explains normalization, continuity, and fixed point, but misses the payoff-improvement role of g_function.
Connects Brouwer.mixedGame, nash_map, and mixed_g_linear at a high level, but misses the pure-deviation contradiction.
Correctly explains internal doors having two incident rooms, fiber size two, and evenness.
Correctly explains the typed nearly colorful versus colorful partition with the same missing type.
Begins the two-doors explanation for nearly colorful rooms, but truncates before the fiber/evenness argument.
Correctly explains TT finite grids, division by l, and the indexed order for Scarf.
Recognizes quantitative bounds and diameter control, but misstates the lemmas and misses the outside-C role.
Explains stabilizing a constant color set, but omits outside-C vanishing and in-C coordinate inequalities.
Correctly explains outside-C zero, sum-one forcing, coordinate inequality, and extensionality.
Correctly explains flat/block indexing, inverse lemmas, and sum/projection/embedding uses.
Correctly explains blockWeight scaling, sum-to-one, and project_embed_id recovery.
Correctly explains division by blockSum, continuity of numerator/denominator, positive denominator, and Continuous.div.
Identifies the tPush case split and blockWeight formula, but truncates before the zero-deficit argument.
Correctly explains mixed_g_linear as the convex-combination bridge from pure to mixed deviations.
Explains transport of fixed points between representations, but misses Fintype.equivFin/ProductSimplices specifics.
Output truncates immediately and does not explain g_function nonnegativity, sum bound, or normalization.
Mentions profitable-deviation sum_g > 1, fixed point, and contradiction, but the fixed-point contradiction mechanism is confused.
